era_context: Fourth Dynasty Egypt under Khafre (or possibly his successor, depending on chronological system). Akkadian Empire in Mesopotamia. Indus Valley Civilization in mature phase. This is the tail end of the Old Kingdom's most prosperous and centralized period.

events.title: Khafre's reign (probable end or continuation)
events.description: Khafre's reign either continues or ends around this period, depending on which chronological system is used. The Fourth Dynasty is characterized by centralized pharaonic power, monumental pyramid construction, and the maintenance of a complex bureaucratic state. Khafre's reign represents the continuation of Old Kingdom stability and prosperity, though some scholars place the beginning of decline toward the end of the Fourth Dynasty.

sources.name: Abydos King List and other Egyptian king lists (later compilations)
Archaeological evidence from Giza and other Old Kingdom sites

events.certainty_note: Khafre's reign is traditionally dated c. 2558–2532 BCE, which would place -2680 near the end of his reign or shortly after. However, chronological uncertainty of ±50–100 years makes year-level precision impossible.

disconfirming_evidence: The dating of Khafre's reign is subject to significant scholarly debate. Different chronological systems ('high,' 'middle,' and 'low' chronologies) place his reign at different absolute dates. Some scholars argue that the Fourth Dynasty ended earlier than traditionally dated, while others extend it. The year -2680 may fall within Khafre's reign, after it, or even before it, depending on the chronological system chosen.
historiographic_note: The historical record for this year is essentially non-existent. We rely entirely on king lists (which are later compilations and may contain errors or gaps) and archaeological evidence (which does not provide year-level precision). The synchronization of Egyptian and Mesopotamian chronologies remains contested. Most ancient years, including this one, are not documented in contemporary sources and cannot be reconstructed with confidence at the year level.

graph_edges.note: Continuation of Fourth Dynasty political and monumental tradition
